rationale.R1.2: A generic section review centrally includes checking whether the argument moves coherently from method and results to conclusions.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes empirical claims about compute reduction and deviation, so review should assess whether those claims align with the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.4: The section makes state-of-the-art and fundamental-shift claims, making novelty assessment a central expected responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: Reviewing a paper section normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.
rationale.R1.7: The section relies on named climate assessment and model references, so citation coverage, attribution, and reference use are central.
rationale.RX.1: Any review projection must account for uncertainty about what can be inferred from the provided section alone.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong deployment and novelty claims, so avoiding overclaiming is active.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay within reviewing the section and not execute edits or broader research tasks.
rationale.RX.4: Because the topic concerns climate-impact decision support, review responsibility includes avoiding downstream harm from unsupported deployment claims.
rationale.RX.5: The review should preserve traceability to claims and evidence present in the artifact.
